title: cross_engine m5 deeper integration plan — m5 ordinal source corpus + descent invariant import into hexa_sim atlas vocabulary
trigger: F127 + F130 cross-engine integration (commit 368209c0) covers 2 m5 file SHAs only (tool/m5_ordinal.py @ F127 + spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt @ F130); m5 ordinal-anchor system internals (5 ω-cycles + dossier + load-bearing descent invariant) remain unanchored. m3 deeper plan (commit e1066dcd) recommends B+C pattern be extended to m5/r4/r10. This is the m5 application.

m5_source_artifacts.role: i1+i2 CNF parser/comparator (Python, ATR_0-anchored, 60 corpus passes)
j1 ordinal grammar BNF (5-line core + 6 side-conditions + sentinel/lex rules)
i1+i2 parser/comparator ω-cycle witness (axes incl. SC1-SC6 canonicality + bare-tower right-assoc)
i5 Kleene fixpoint detector ω-cycle (3-window plateau detection → m9 rollback trigger)
i6 23-round (R4..R41) back-stamp migration ω-cycle (ω, ω+1, .., ω+37 under PRA)
m5 infra implementation ω-cycle (parent of i1..i6 sub-cycles, dossier-bound)
m5 design dossier (Γ_0 anchor, 9 sections, F-m5-A..D falsifier specs, ATR_0 escape_basis)

m5_invariants_identified_from_dossier_an: I1 ordinal_descent_strict_gentzen_terminator — ascending convention: round n+1 ordinal_label >_CNF round n's; strict descent claim (is_strict_descent=true) requires fresh axes_acted (not noop-only); falsifier F-m5-A; theorem: Gentzen 1936 (descending sequence in well-ordered set is finite)
I2 cnf_canonicality_sc1_strict_descent_in_sum — sum exponents strictly decreasing β_i > β_{i+1}; SC1 violation = NonCanonicalCNFError; per spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt SC1
I3 escape_basis_at_least_axis_ordinal — ordinal_label ε_0 with escape_basis PA = falsified (Gentzen: PA cannot witness its own proof-theoretic ordinal); falsifier F-m5-C; ladder PRA→PA→ATR_0→Π¹₁-CA₀

I4 fixpoint_3_window_kleene_signature — 3 consecutive rounds with identical ordinal_label = m17 Kleene self-loop; emit fixpoint_warning + m9 atomic-rollback; F-m5-D fires on 3-plateau without rollback
I5 axes_acted_nonempty_when_strict_descent_claimed — is_strict_descent=true requires len(axes_acted)>0; cosmetic-only delta with descent claim = lying ordinal stamp; F-m5-A core check
I6 sentinel_arithmetic_blocked — sentinel + tail / sentinel · n raise OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError; SN3 in BNF spec; sentinels (ε_0/Γ_0/ψ(Ω_ω)) standalone only (SC4)

I7 homoglyph_unicode_strict_lx_codepoints — LX1-LX4 enforce NFC + reject U+00A0/U+2009/U+200B + lock ω=U+03C9 ε=U+03B5 Γ=U+0393 ψ=U+03C8 Ω=U+03A9 · =U+00B7; HomoglyphRejected on Latin/Cyrillic confusables

candidate_categorization_for_atlas.OPT_A_schema_field_S_entries: m5 evolution_log row schema fields (ordinal_label/predecessor/is_strict_descent/escape_basis) → @S entries. Verdict: REJECTED — F162 SHA-anchors the i4 migration ω-cycle which embeds the field spec; @S field-level entries duplicate without verifier leverage (analog of m3 OPT-A rejection).
candidate_categorization_for_atlas.OPT_B_invariant_M_meta_entries: m5 phase invariants → @M meta-axis entries. Each invariant becomes one @M entry in NEW shard atlas.append.m5-deeper-2026-04-26.n6 with grep-based falsifier. I1 (descent + Gentzen termination) is the load-bearing invariant — m5's reason-for-existing per dossier §5. Verdict: SELECTED for I1 as the headline meta-axis (analog of F132 + F160).
candidate_categorization_for_atlas.OPT_C_phase_T_trace_entries: m5 ω-cycles + dossier → @T file-SHA entries. @T m5_i1i2_parser_comparator_omega_v0 / @T m5_i4_schema_migration_omega_v0 / @T m5_i5_fixpoint_detector_omega_v0 / @T m5_i6_23_round_migration_omega_v0 / @T m5_ordinal_infra_omega_v0 / @T m5_ordinal_anchor_dossier_v0. Each SHA-stable, idempotent. Verdict: SELECTED — covers 6 m5 source files not in F127/F130.
candidate_categorization_for_atlas.OPT_D_extra_specs_corpus_fuzzer_errors: spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv + spec/m5_ordinal_fuzzer_corpus.tsv + spec/m5_error_classes.txt + tool/m5_ordinal_test.py + design/meta_engine/2026-04-26_m5_spec_corpus_omega_cycle.json + 2026-04-26_m5_bnf_augment_omega_cycle.json + 2026-04-26_m5_i1i2_code_emission_omega_cycle.json. Verdict: DEFERRED Tier-2 — F127 PROBE_RUN already covers test PASS (which exercises corpus + fuzzer + error classes transitively); test SHA derives from tool SHA via PASS evidence; spec_corpus / bnf_augment / i1i2_emission ω-cycles are post-hoc surface ω-cycles whose SHA-anchoring would inflate axis count past the F126-F132 cadence — better held until next ω-pass.
candidate_categorization_for_atlas.chosen: B + C combination — 6 @T file-SHA entries (m5 corpus deeper) + 1 @M meta-axis (I1 descent + Gentzen terminator, the load-bearing invariant). Total 7 atlas entries (matches F126-F132 + m3-deeper F154-F160 cadence). Other I-invariants (I2..I7) deferred to Tier-2: SC1 canonicality + escape_basis ladder + fixpoint window + axes_acted + sentinel arithmetic + homoglyph rules — all need impl-level instrumented runs (parser exception traces or evolution_log row scans) not yet on disk; SHA-only anchoring would be premature, but F161 + F166 together SHA-anchor the spec text where these invariants are formally stated.
